title: rosa pomifera subvar. gaudinii
intro: Rosa pomifera subvar. gaudinii, also known as the Rosa gaudinii or wild apple rose, is a shrub-like plant native to the mountainous regions of China. The plant has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects, which contribute to its potential health benefits such as reducing fever, alleviating pain, and promoting wound healing. Herbal preparations of Rosa pomifera subvar. gaudinii include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions. However, more research is needed to fully understand the efficacy and safety of using this plant for medicinal purposes.
